text: L-Methionine, chemical formula C5H11NO2S, molecular weight 149.21, is a single-component amino acid organic molecular crystal. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group P21 (No. 4), with unit cell a = 9.512 Å, b = 5.209 Å, c = 14.858 Å, β = 99.703°, Z = 4. Molecules are connected by hydrogen bonding interactions; the non-centrosymmetric polar structure is compatible with piezoelectric properties. The crystal structure (CCDC 276855, CSD refcode LMETON11) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 258 K with an R factor of 5.29% and a calculated density of 1.366 g/cm³. Recent experimental studies have demonstrated piezoelectric behavior in L-methionine crystals using piezoelectric nanogenerator devices. Sublimated polycrystalline films of L-Methionine show enhanced piezoelectric performance with oriented crystal growth along the b-axis, achieving a maximum piezoelectric response of 6.57 pC/N and voltage outputs exceeding 8 V. The crystals exhibit low cross-sample variability and reproducible behavior, making them suitable for piezoelectric sensing and energy harvesting applications.
property_ref_title: Sublimation of orientated amino acid films for reliable, amplified piezoelectric performance

sublimation_parameters.repetitivity: high - low standard deviation across samples

mechanical_properties.stress_strain_response: near-continuous linear response up to 100% strain
mechanical_properties.flexibility: adjustable by controlling sublimation temperature and time

additional_notes: Non-centrosymmetric polar monoclinic space group (P21) is crystallographically compatible with piezoelectricity
Sublimated films exceed performance of single crystals in some conditions
Low cross-sample variability compared to conventional solution-based crystallization
Crystal structure consists of discrete neutral L-methionine molecules held together by hydrogen bonding
Experimental studies have demonstrated piezoelectric energy harvesting using L-methionine crystals in nanogenerator devices
Oriented L-methionine thin films show enhanced piezoelectric performance
Sublimation is a solvent-free 'green' alternative suitable for industrial scale-up
Natural self-assembly results in crystals oriented perpendicular to growth substrate, maximizing piezoelectric output
